license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.fi2bkge
title: Sampling to Monitor Fluorescence during Cell Lysis: Large-scale One-step Phage Infection of Cyanobacteria
author: Sarah Giuliani

steps_raw.instruction: Before sampling, add 180 ul ASW into the wells of a 96-well plate labeled as a fluorescence plate. Plate-Setup: a
Add 20 ul of unfiltered sample into the ASW for a 10X dilution. Mix well with a pipette.

For filtered samples, transfer 280 ul from each experiment bottle or sample tube into a 96-well filter plate stacked onto a bottom 96-well filtrate plate, and centrifuge the plates at 1000 X g for 3 minutes.
From the filtrate, add 20 ul of sample into the ASW for a 10X dilution. Mix well with a pipette.

Take fluorescence readings using the “chla + phyco_onestep.prt” program on the Tecan plate reader, which reads fluorescence at 680 nm (excitation 440 nm) for chlorophyll a and reads fluorescence at 577 nm (excitation at 544 nm) for phycoerythrin.
Plot the fluorescence vs. time and use this to track the real-time progression of the infection.
